statement: Filer ships with a curated library of template nodes that any project can install as a starting point.
reason: Filer's value depends on having useful nodes. A user who runs `filer init` against an empty repo gets an empty .filer/ directory and no obvious next step. Bundling a starter library lowers the time-to-first-value from hours to seconds, and demonstrates the shape of well-formed nodes by example.

alternatives_rejected.option: Generate templates per-project via LLM (the Continue Checks model)
alternatives_rejected.why_rejected: Requires an LLM call at install time, which means an API key, latency, and provider lock-in. A bundled library works offline, deterministically, and on day zero of a project before any LLM has been configured.

alternatives_rejected.option: Ship as a separate npm package (@filer/templates)
alternatives_rejected.why_rejected: Separate package means a separate install step, separate version cycle, and separate discovery. Bundling in @filer/cli adds ~50KB and removes all friction. Can be extracted later if the library grows.

revisit_if: Template count exceeds 100 (consider extraction), or per-project LLM generation becomes meaningfully better than curation.
